content:
# Numbers Chapter 31 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 186-188 | **Source Image:** `NUM_031_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_031_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_031_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0137-pa_0138

**1.** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**2.** "Take vengeance for the sons of Israel against the Midianites; afterward you shall be gathered to your people."

**3.** And Moses spoke to the people, saying, "Arm men from among you for war, and let them go against Midian to execute the vengeance of YHWH upon Midian.

**4.** One thousand from each tribe, one thousand from each tribe, from all the tribes of Israel you shall send to the war."

**5.** And there were mustered from the thousands of Israel one thousand from each tribe, twelve thousand armed for war.

**6.** And Moses sent them — one thousand from each tribe — to the war, them and Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest to the war, with the holy vessels and the trumpets for the alarm blast in his hand.

**7.** And they warred against Midian, as YHWH commanded Moses, and they killed every male.

**8.** And they killed the kings of Midian along with their slain: Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba — five kings of Midian; and Balaam the son of Beor they killed with the sword.

**9.** And the sons of Israel took captive the women of Midian and their little ones, and all their cattle and all their livestock and all their wealth they plundered.

**10.** And all their cities in their dwelling places and all their encampments they burned with fire.

**11.** And they took all the spoil and all the plunder, both of man and of beast.

**12.** And they brought the captives and the plunder and the spoil to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the congregation of the sons of Israel, to the camp, to the plains of Moab which are by the Jordan at Jericho.

**13.** And Moses and Eleazar the priest and all the leaders of the congregation went out to meet them outside the camp.

**14.** And Moses was angry with the officers of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, who came from the service of the war.

**15.** And Moses said to them, "Have you kept alive every woman?

**16.** Behold, these are the ones who caused the sons of Israel, by the word of Balaam, to commit unfaithfulness against YHWH in the matter of Peor, so that the plague came upon the congregation of YHWH.

**17.** And now, kill every male among the little ones, and kill every woman who has known a man by lying with a male.

**18.** But all the young girls who have not known lying with a male, keep alive for yourselves.

**19.** And you, encamp outside the camp seven days; everyone who has killed a person and everyone who has touched a slain one — purify yourselves on the third day and on the seventh day, you and your captives.

**20.** And every garment and every article of skin and every work of goats' hair and every article of wood you shall purify."

**21.** And Eleazar the priest said to the men of war who had gone to the battle, "This is the statute of the law which YHWH commanded Moses:

**22.** Only the gold and the silver, the bronze, the iron, the tin, and the lead —

**23.** everything that comes into fire you shall pass through fire, and it shall be clean; nevertheless it shall be purified with the water of impurity. And everything that does not come into fire you shall pass through water.

**24.** And you shall wash your garments on the seventh day, and you shall be clean; and afterward you may come into the camp."

**25.** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**26.** "Take the sum of the plunder of the captives, both of man and of beast, you and Eleazar the priest and the heads of the fathers of the congregation.

**27.** And divide the plunder into two parts, between those skilled in war who went out to battle and all the congregation.

**28.** And you shall levy a tribute for YHWH from the men of war who went out to battle: one soul out of five hundred, from the persons, and from the cattle, and from the donkeys, and from the flock.

**29.** From their half you shall take it and give it to Eleazar the priest as a contribution to YHWH.

**30.** And from the half belonging to the sons of Israel you shall take one portion out of fifty, from the persons, from the cattle, from the donkeys, and from the flock — from all the livestock — and you shall give them to the Levites who keep the charge of the tabernacle of YHWH."

**31.** And Moses and Eleazar the priest did as YHWH commanded Moses.

**32.** And the plunder, the remainder of the spoil which the people of the war had plundered, was: six hundred and seventy-five thousand sheep,

**33.** and seventy-two thousand cattle,

**34.** and sixty-one thousand donkeys,

**35.** and the persons — from the women who had not known lying with a male — all the persons were thirty-two thousand.

**36.** And the half, the portion of those who went out to war, the number of sheep was three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred;

**37.** and the tribute to YHWH from the sheep was six hundred and seventy-five.

**38.** And the cattle were thirty-six thousand, and their tribute to YHWH was seventy-two.

**39.** And the donkeys were thirty thousand five hundred, and their tribute to YHWH was sixty-one.

**40.** And the persons were sixteen thousand, and their tribute to YHWH was thirty-two persons.

**41.** And Moses gave the tribute, the contribution of YHWH, to Eleazar the priest, as YHWH commanded Moses.

**42.** And from the half belonging to the sons of Israel, which Moses divided from the men who had gone to war —

**43.** now the half belonging to the congregation was: three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,

**44.** and thirty-six thousand cattle,

**45.** and thirty thousand five hundred donkeys,

**46.** and sixteen thousand persons —

**47.** and from the half belonging to the sons of Israel, Moses took one portion out of fifty, from the persons and from the livestock, and gave them to the Levites who keep the charge of the tabernacle of YHWH, as YHWH commanded Moses.

**48.** And the officers who were over the thousands of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, approached Moses.

**49.** And they said to Moses, "Your servants have taken the sum of the men of war who were under our hand, and not a man is missing from us.

**50.** And we have brought the offering of YHWH, what each man found: articles of gold — armlets and bracelets, signet rings, earrings, and pendants — to make atonement for our souls before YHWH."

**51.** And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from them, every crafted article.

**52.** And all the gold of the contribution which they contributed to YHWH was sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels, from the commanders of thousands and from the commanders of hundreds.

**53.** The men of war had plundered, each man for himself.

**54.** And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and they brought it into the tent of meeting as a memorial for the sons of Israel before YHWH.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **v.2 — "Take vengeance" (נְקֹם נִקְמַת):** The Hebrew uses a cognate accusative construction, literally "avenge the vengeance of." The formal rendering "take vengeance" preserves the imperative force while the phrase "for the sons of Israel" renders the לִבְנֵי construction indicating the vengeance is executed on their behalf.

2. **v.4 — "One thousand from each tribe, one thousand from each tribe" (אֶלֶף לַמַּטֶּה אֶלֶף לַמַּטֶּה):** The repetition is preserved exactly as it appears in the Hebrew, emphasizing the equal mustering from every tribe.

3. **v.6 — "holy vessels" (כְּלֵי הַקֹּדֶשׁ):** The MT reads "vessels of the holiness," likely referring to sacred cultic objects carried into battle. The LXX (Vaticanus) reads τὰ σκεύη τὰ ἅγια, confirming "the holy vessels." Some traditions identify these with the Ark or the Urim and Thummim, but the text does not specify.

4. **v.8 — Five kings of Midian:** The five names (Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, Reba) correspond to those listed in Joshua 13:21 as vassals of Sihon. Zur is identified in Numbers 25:15 as the father of Cozbi.

5. **v.16 — "by the word of Balaam" (בִּדְבַר בִּלְעָם):** This phrase attributes the Midianite strategy at Peor (ch. 25) to Balaam's counsel. The LXX renders ἐν τῷ ῥήματι Βαλααμ, confirming the Hebrew reading.

6. **v.17 — "lying with a male" (מִשְׁכַּב זָכָר):** Literally "the lying-down of a male." This technical phrase distinguishes sexual experience and appears also in v.18 and v.35.

7. **v.23 — "water of impurity" (מֵי נִדָּה):** This refers to the lustral water prepared with the ashes of the red heifer as prescribed in Numbers 19. The term נִדָּה denotes ritual separation/impurity. The Vaticanus reads ὕδατι ἁγνισμοῦ ("water of purification"), interpreting the purpose rather than the literal meaning.

8. **v.28 — "one soul out of five hundred" (אֶחָד נֶפֶשׁ מֵחֲמֵשׁ הַמֵּאוֹת):** The tribute rate from the warriors' half (1:500) contrasts with the rate from the congregation's half (1:50, v.30), the warriors giving proportionally less since they bore the risk of battle.

9. **v.50 — Listing of gold articles:** The Hebrew lists five types: אֶצְעָדָה (armlet/ankle-chain), צָמִיד (bracelet), טַבַּעַת (signet ring), עָגִיל (earring), and כוּמָז (pendant/ornament). The last term (כוּמָז) is rare, appearing only here and in Exodus 35:22; its exact nature is debated.

10. **v.50 — "to make atonement for our souls" (לְכַפֵּר עַל־נַפְשֹׁתֵינוּ):** The officers offer gold as a ransom or atonement payment. This voluntary offering parallels the half-shekel ransom of Exodus 30:12-16, suggesting awareness that survival in battle requires expiation before YHWH.

11. **v.52 — "sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels":** This totals approximately 190 kilograms (420 lbs) of gold. The Hebrew specifies שְׁקָלִים as the unit of weight.

12. **v.54 — "memorial" (זִכָּרוֹן):** The gold placed in the tent of meeting serves as a "memorial" (remembrance) before YHWH, a permanent testimony to divine deliverance in battle. The LXX renders μνημόσυνον, confirming the memorial function.

13. **MT-LXX Agreement:** The Vaticanus text of Numbers 31 closely follows the Hebrew in both content and sequence. No significant textual variants were observed that would affect the translation. The verse numbering and narrative structure are consistent between the traditions.
